transcript.clean_text: welcome back to our dura italia coverage here on gcn and eurosport and to stage five a day that couldn't have been more contrasting to yesterday's cold wet and hilly affair today blue skies mild temperatures and just 200 meters of climbing over 177 k's they set out from modena would head south east through bologna then through immolar host to the uci world champs last autumn and eventually to the adriatic coast and the finish line in catalina with the day almost certain to end in a bunch sprint there were few riders interested in expending energy in an early break only two in fact umberto marengo of bardiani csf followed a move made by felipe tadliani of androni geocataly cedarmech enjoying his day in pink was alessandro dimaki of the israel startup nation who was clearly a popular wearer of it a moment i'm sure he will never forget the gap to the two leaders had been slashed by the time they reached the first intermediate sprint tadliani took the maximum points but just a few seconds further back it was gaviria viviani and malia who take third fourth and fifth respectively soon after that with a chance of crosswinds the two riders out front were caught despite the obvious nervousness in the bunch though nothing of any significance occurred for the next hour or two that was until the same two teams attacked with 68k to go different riders this time simon paulo of androni joined by davide gebura of bardiani and it was the latter who would roll through the second and final intermediate sprint in front they'd eventually have company though alexey gujjar of adidas situate had obviously got board in the bunch and set off in pursuit just after the 25k to go banner so two eventually became three but with just 23 seconds of an advantage they stood little chance against the sprinters teams behind back in the bunch the nervousness continued and it caused the unfortunate but almost inevitable crash the man involved pavel sivakov of the ineos grenadiers he would finish the stage but 13 minutes down joe dombrowski also had a nasty fool just under five k's to go he too would finish the stage but unfortunately mikel lander didn't the basque rider finished his jiro italia in an ambulance not something any of us wanted to see the stage win was still up for grabs though and it would be the sprinters who had their second chance of the race the break court with just over three k's to go alperson fenix looking to deliver tim malia to a second stage victory krieger leading him out but it was coffees who came up on the right hand side of the road like bullets are coming together with ewing and a drop chain ended malia's chances but not ewan's the australian was left with a lot of work to do but somehow he managed to get past both viviani and nizolo before the line to take his fourth career giro stage win the overhead shot shows how tight the gaps were and also how long nizola was sprinting into the wind he went on completely the opposite side of the road and almost held on only to be thwarted by ewan on the line that was nizola's 11th second place at the gerald italia and his only consolation is that he now leads the points classification so he'll be in the chicklemino jersey tomorrow behind those three it was segan in fourth place on the day then gaviria moshketti patrick segafredo and sixth in pascual gruner vegan boletti and chimalai in the top 10. a few ambitions having to be reset for tomorrow borrowing victorious and here's hoping that joe dombrowski isn't too badly hurt no change in the pink jersey demaki finished safe and sound in the bunch to conserve that but with dombrowski losing time and lander out of the race there has been a change behind him in the gc ivaca up to second place for alps in phoenix then valtter then it's a day vlas vlassoff avenue paul betyal and carthy and then bernal rounding out the top 10 as things stand behind them we have carruzzo still up there for bahrain victorious he could be their leader now former low in 12th moscow moscon's still up there in 15th post aviva dan martin and martinez rounding out the top 20. we're back to the climbs tomorrow stage six takes the race from the grotto di flasarti to ascoli piccino over 160 k's just over midway through the stage they'll reach the top of the second category climate of the fork of the gulda and soon after that the highest point of the giro so far from there it's a long 44k descent to the foot of the final climb that's where the intermediate sprint is located 15 and a half kilometers all uphill so there's sure to be more changes in the gc find out what those are tomorrow on gcm plus and eurosport where you can catch live coverage territory restrictions apply but if you can join us please do we'll see you then you
